data.content:
Here's my analysis of the legacy patterns and how to modernize:

## Legacy Patterns Identified

| # | Legacy Pattern | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Non-SDK format (`ToolsVersion`, XML namespace, explicit `Import` of props/targets) | Use `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `<Compile Include="..."/>` for every .cs file | SDK-style auto-globs all `.cs` files |
| 3 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` with hand-maintained attributes | Move metadata to `<PropertyGroup>` in .csproj; delete file |
| 4 | Explicit `<Reference>` for System assemblies | Implicit with SDK-style targeting `net472` |
| 5 | Debug/Release `<PropertyGroup>` boilerplate (OutputPath, DebugType, etc.) | SDK defaults handle these |
| 6 | `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic` | All unnecessary/default in SDK-style |
| 7 | `TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2 | Use `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |

## Modernized .csproj

The entire 47-line file reduces to:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Additionally**, `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` can be deleted since the SDK auto-generates assembly attributes from the csproj properties.

Would you like me to apply these changes?
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize it to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns identified:
1. **Non-SDK project format** - Uses `ToolsVersion`, XML namespace, and explicit `Import` statements for `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets`
2. **Explicit file includes** - `<Compile Include="..."/>` for each .cs file (SDK-style uses globbing automatically)
3. **AssemblyInfo.cs** - Manually managed assembly attributes in `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` (SDK-style auto-generates these)


4. **Framework references** - Explicit `<Reference>` items for System assemblies that are unnecessary in modern SDK projects
5. **Configuration boilerplate** - Debug/Release property groups with redundant defaults that SDK-style handles automatically
6. **Unnecessary metadata** - ProjectGuid, FileAlignment, and other properties that either aren't needed or have sensible defaults in SDK-style projects
7. **Outdated framework targeting** - Using TargetFrameworkVersion instead of the modern TargetFramework property with the new TFM format

The modernized version would be far more concise. I'll convert this by mapping `v4.7.2` to `net472` in the new format, moving AssemblyInfo attributes into the project file itself, and stripping out all the boilerplate that modern tooling handles automatically.
